pages.raw_text:
The employment agency license
Of Cary, Buck & Hill, S1S Stevens
street, was cancelled today by
Mayor Pratt. Later he had MIR.
Hill of the employment firm ar
rested for selling job to Clarence
Owens. Hill was placed under q
$500 bond.


The order cancelling the license
was signed last night, but the en-
ployment office was closed and the
paper could not be served Bright
and early this morning Attorney
Settlers appeared before the super
Tor court to secure temporary re.
straining order to prevent the
mayor from revoking the license.


He was too late, however, for
when he arrived at the mayors of.
flee the notification of revocation
had been served by the company.


short time afterward Hill sold a
Job to Clarence Owens and was ar
rested and taken to Jail.


Bond was fixed at $500 and the
man remained in custody more
than an hour before his friends
came to his rescue with the nee
cry coin.


This agency, it is said by the
mayors office, has been causing
much trouble of late, eliminating
the other day when they sent seven
men to Lind, and refusing to pay
their expense, in the neighborhood
of $72, when the men returned
here, reporting their inability to
get work.


Boppel & Bisby, the contractors
at Lind, have a standing order for
from 10 to Is men a day at their
camp, when, as a matter of fact
they only employ II men. The
mayor believes that the contractors
and employment agents have been
sending men to Lind and dividing
the fees.


81 intend to put stop to the
practice of these employment
agents of beating men out of money
